headline: James answered with concrete needs for authority and criteria, but engagement and clarity are sharply low since the promotion decision.
summary_bullets: James’ engagement dip shows up as “less present in rituals,” and it ties directly to the promotion moment rather than day-to-day workload.
He didn’t absorb actionable promotion feedback; he “was not clear on what really needed to change,” and then chose delivery because it felt controllable.
His mentoring is a bright spot for usefulness, but he still doubts recognition: “I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.”
understanding_paragraph: James is not primarily asking for reassurance—he’s asking for decision-grade clarity: criteria, authority, and a precise path. The promotion decision left him unclear on what needed to change, so he defaulted to controllable delivery and pulled back from team rituals. Mentoring remains where he feels useful, but he still can’t connect his current work to what will be recognized next.

axes.meaning: Weak signal: small stress/energy drag tied to needing clearer criteria, not burnout-level exhaustion.
A real engagement drop: he’s less present in rituals and the promotion aftermath is draining investment.
Defining signal: he lacks clear criteria and real authority, so priorities and expectations aren’t landing.
Weak signal: he can still focus on delivery, but growth direction isn’t clearly established.

brutal_truth_employee: James explicitly links the shift to the promotion: “After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.” He also names the core blocker as authority and recognition: “I can own bigger direction, but I need real authority to do it” and “I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.”
brutal_truth_manager: You got strong, specific signals, and James gave you a clear through-line: criteria/authority after the promotion. The next risk is letting the conversation stop at “define the path precisely” without turning it into an observable set of criteria and decisions you own—because his clarity answer is absolute: “need real authority.”

next_actions.action: Schedule a 30-minute follow-up with James to define the exact criteria and authority boundaries for the next 4–6 weeks, then confirm them in writing.
Offer one concrete forum James named (or choose one together) and set a date to review whether his mentoring work maps to what gets recognized.
Start next 1:1 by asking James to name one ritual he will rejoin and what “clear criteria” would look like there, then capture the commitment.

watch_for: Within two weeks, if James still says he is “not clear on what really needed to change” after you define criteria, clarity work is failing.
Over the next month, if James remains “less present in rituals” even after authority/criteria are clarified, engagement is still tied to the promotion outcome.
